rules.title: Slovak Ordinal Numbers - Overview
rules.text: Slovak ordinal numbers (radové číslovky) express position or order in a sequence and answer the question 'ktorý?' (which one?). Unlike cardinals, ALL ordinals decline like adjectives with full gender, number, and case agreement. They describe position (first, second, third) rather than quantity. Ordinals are formed from cardinal numbers with specific suffixes and always agree with the noun they modify.
examples: prvý muž (the first man - masculine)
druhá žena (the second woman - feminine)
tretie dieťa (the third child - neuter)
Vidím prvého muža (I see the first man - accusative)
S piatym mužom (with the fifth man - instrumental)

rules.title: Ordinal Number 'prvý' (first) - Complete Declension
rules.text: 'Prvý' (first) is the most common ordinal and declines like a hard-stem adjective. It shows full gender agreement (masculine, feminine, neuter) and distinguishes between animate and inanimate in masculine accusative.
examples: Nominative: Prvý muž prišiel - The first man came
Nominative: Prvá žena odišla - The first woman left
Nominative: Prvé dieťa spí - The first child is sleeping
Genitive: Dom prvého muža - The first man's house
Genitive: Kniha prvej ženy - The first woman's book
Dative: Prvému mužovi - To the first man
Accusative: Vidím prvého muža - I see the first man (animate)
Accusative: Vidím prvý dom - I see the first house (inanimate)
Accusative: Vidím prvú ženu - I see the first woman
Locative: O prvom mužovi - About the first man
Instrumental: S prvým mužom - With the first man
Instrumental: S prvou ženou - With the first woman

rules.title: Ordinal Number 'druhý' (second) - Complete Declension
rules.text: 'Druhý' (second) follows the same declension pattern as 'prvý'. It's a hard-stem adjective with full gender and case agreement.
examples: Nominative: Druhý muž - The second man
Nominative: Druhá kniha - The second book
Nominative: Druhé auto - The second car
Genitive: Cena druhého domu - The price of the second house
Dative: Druhému študentovi - To the second student
Accusative: Vidím druhého muža - I see the second man
Locative: O druhom dome - About the second house
Instrumental: S druhým mužom - With the second man

rules.title: Ordinal Number 'tretí' (third) - Complete Declension
rules.text: 'Tretí' (third) is a soft-stem adjective, so it has slightly different endings than 'prvý' and 'druhý'. Note the -í ending in nominative masculine and the soft consonant throughout.
examples: Nominative: Tretí muž - The third man
Nominative: Tretia žena - The third woman
Nominative: Tretie dieťa - The third child
Genitive: Dom tretieho muža - The third man's house
Dative: Tretiemu mužovi - To the third man
Accusative: Vidím tretieho muža - I see the third man
Locative: O treťom mužovi - About the third man
Instrumental: S tretím mužom - With the third man

rules.title: Ordinal Numbers 4th-10th - Complete Declension Table
rules.text: Ordinals 4-10 are hard-stem adjectives ending in -ý (masculine nominative). They all follow the same pattern as 'prvý' and 'druhý'.
examples: Nominative: Štvrtý deň - The fourth day
Nominative: Piata hodina - The fifth hour
Nominative: Šieste poschodie - The sixth floor
Genitive: Cena siedmeho domu - The price of the seventh house
Dative: Ôsmemu študentovi - To the eighth student
Accusative: Vidím deviateho muža - I see the ninth man
Locative: O desiatom dome - About the tenth house
Instrumental: S piatym mužom - With the fifth man

rules.title: Ordinal Numbers 11th-20th - Complete Declension Table
rules.text: Ordinals 11-20 are formed from cardinals with -y/-ý suffix. They all decline like hard-stem adjectives.
examples: Jedenásty deň - The eleventh day
Dvanásta hodina - The twelfth hour
V trinástom dome - In the thirteenth house
S pätnástym študentom - With the fifteenth student
Dvadsiaty storočie - The twentieth century

rules.title: Ordinal Numbers 20th-100th - Tens Declension
rules.text: Ordinals for tens (20th, 30th, etc.) are formed from cardinal tens with -y suffix. They decline like hard-stem adjectives.
examples: Dvadsiaty rok - The twentieth year
Tridsiata minúta - The thirtieth minute
Päťdesiaty deň - The fiftieth day
Stý ročník - The one hundredth anniversary
V stom dome - In the one hundredth house

rules.title: Ordinal Numbers - Gender Agreement
rules.text: All ordinals must agree with the noun in gender (masculine, feminine, neuter). Here are the three genders for common ordinals in nominative case.

rows: prvý muž / prvá žena / prvé dieťa
druhý dom / druhá kniha / druhé auto
tretí deň / tretia ulica / tretie poschodie
štvrtý mesiac / štvrtá trieda / štvrté miesto
piaty rok / piata hodina / piate dieťa
desiaty ročník / desiata strana / desiate číslo
dvadsiaty storočie / dvadsiata minúta / dvadsiate výročie
stý deň / stá strana / sté výročie

examples: Prvý muž prišiel - The first man came
Prvá žena odišla - The first woman left
Prvé dieťa spí - The first child is sleeping
Druhá kniha je lepšia - The second book is better
Tretie poschodie - The third floor
Piata hodina - The fifth hour
Desiata strana - The tenth page

rules.title: Compound Ordinal Numbers - Formation Rules
rules.text: Compound ordinals (21st-99th, 101st+) are formed by combining tens and units. Only the last element is ordinal; earlier elements remain as cardinals. Both parts decline when the ordinal is in oblique cases.
examples: Dvadsiatyprvý deň - The twenty-first day
Dvadsiatypiaty marec - The twenty-fifth of March
Tridsiaty prvý december - The thirty-first of December
Stý ročník - The one hundredth anniversary
O dvadsiatom prvom - About the twenty-first
S dvadsiatym piatym - With the twenty-fifth

rules.title: Special Ordinals - 'prvý' and 'posledný'
rules.text: 'Prvý' (first) and 'posledný' (last) are the most commonly used ordinals. 'Posledný' (last) declines like a regular hard-stem adjective.
examples: Prvý deň - The first day
Prvá láska - First love
Posledný vlak - The last train
Posledná šanca - The last chance
Ďalší týždeň - Next week
Predchádzajúci mesiac - Previous month
Na prvom mieste - In first place
Do posledného - To the last one

rules.title: Ordinals as Nouns - Substantive Use
rules.text: Ordinals frequently function as nouns (substantives) when they stand alone without modifying another noun. They decline like adjectives and take appropriate gender based on what they refer to.

rows: The first one is the best
I spoke with the first one

examples: Prvý je najlepší - The first one is the best
Druhá prišla neskoro - The second one (female) came late
Tretí vyhral - The third one won
Hovoril som s prvým - I spoke with the first one
Videl som druhého - I saw the second one
Piaty v rade - The fifth in line
Desiaty z nich - The tenth of them
Prvá z nich - The first one (female) of them

rules.title: Ordinals in Dates
rules.text: Ordinals are used for days of the month in dates. They appear in genitive case when expressing 'on the [date]'. Months are also in genitive.
examples: Prvého januára - On the first of January
Dvadsiateho marca - On the twentieth of March
Tridsiateho prvého decembra - On the thirty-first of December
Narodil sa prvého mája - He was born on the first of May
Dvadsiateho druhého marca 2026 - On March 22, 2026
V roku 2026 - In the year 2026 (cardinal for year)

rules.title: Ordinals in Time Expressions
rules.text: Ordinals can be used for hours of the day, though cardinals are more common. When used, they appear in locative case with 'o' (at).
examples: O prvej hodine - At one o'clock (locative)
O druhej - At two o'clock
O piatej - At five o'clock
O dvanástej - At twelve o'clock
Prvá hodina - One o'clock (nominative)
Je prvá hodina - It's one o'clock

rules.title: Ordinals with Fractions
rules.text: Ordinals are used to form fraction nouns. The ordinal becomes a feminine noun ending in -ina.

rows: štvrtina hodiny (a quarter of an hour)

examples: Polovica chleba - Half of the bread
Tretina študentov - A third of the students
Štvrtina hodiny - A quarter of an hour
Pätina obyvateľov - A fifth of the inhabitants
Desatina úrody - A tenth of the harvest

rules.title: Ordinals in Titles and Names
rules.text: Ordinals are used in royal titles, pope names, and historical periods. They follow the noun and agree in gender and case.
examples: Ján Pavol Druhý - John Paul II (the Second)
Alžbeta Druhá - Elizabeth II
Karol Piaty - Charles V
Prvá svetová vojna - World War I (First World War)
Druhá svetová vojna - World War II (Second World War)
Dvadsiaty prvý storočie - The twenty-first century

rules.title: Summary - Ordinal vs Cardinal Behavior
rules.text: Quick reference comparing how ordinals and cardinals behave grammatically in Slovak.

rows: 1 = Nom sg; 2-4 = Nom pl; 5+ = Gen pl
